mnemonics: Lazy Parents Prioritize Sleep (Largest Priority Sleeps): For a Max-Heap, the parent node is always larger than its children, so it can be 'lazy' while the children do the work during heapify. The largest element (highest priority) gets to 'sleep' at the root.
keyPoints: A heap is a complete binary tree that satisfies the heap property (max-heap: parent >= children, min-heap: parent <= children).
Heapsort uses a max-heap to sort by repeatedly extracting the maximum element and placing it at the end of the array, then heapifying the reduced heap.
The key operations are build-max-heap (O(n)) and max-heapify (O(log n)), leading to an overall O(n log n) time complexity for heapsort.
commonMistakes: Forgetting that heap indices typically start at 1, not 0, leading to off-by-one errors when calculating parent/child positions (e.g., parent(i) = i/2, left(i)=2i).
Confusing the heap property with the binary search tree property (left < parent < right); in a heap, there is no ordering between left and right children, only between parent and children.
